About

My background combines campus IT operations, user support, security-focused troubleshooting, and hands-on defensive labs. I care about practical security work: understanding alerts, documenting facts clearly, separating assumptions from evidence, and helping teams respond safely.

Security Operations Mindset

Focused on phishing analysis, suspicious login investigation, endpoint alert review, vulnerability prioritization, and clear ticket documentation.

AI for Defensive Workflows

Building safe AI-assisted tools that support analysts without replacing human judgment or connecting directly to production systems too early.

Hands-On Learning

Projects use real tools and reproducible documentation: Wazuh, Sysmon, Kali, Python, AWS, SQL, Streamlit, XGBoost, and MITRE ATT&CK mapping.

Selected Projects

Hands-on projects that demonstrate defensive security, alert triage, AI safety, cloud analytics, and technical communication. Each project is framed around safe, practical security outcomes.

Real lab screenshot: Wazuh dashboard, Windows target environment, and attacker workstation.

Air-Gapped SOC & Active Defense Lab

Published

Built an isolated cyber lab connecting a Kali attacker VM, a physical Windows target, Sysmon telemetry, and Wazuh SIEM. Simulated reconnaissance and defense evasion, then mapped detections to MITRE ATT&CK.

AI Prompt Injection Simulation

Published

Demonstrated OWASP LLM01 prompt injection against a mock AI SOC log analyzer, then patched it with defensive prompt architecture, strict data delimiting, and guardrails.
